definitions.definition: A differential equation is an equation that involves an unknown function and its derivatives.
The order of a differential equation is the order of the highest order derivative of the unknown function.
The degree of a differential equation is the power to which the highest order derivative is raised.
A differential equation is said to be linear if it is of the form f(x,y)dx + g(x,y)ydy=0 where f(x,y) and g(x,y) are functions of x and y only.

definitions.term: Linear Differential Equation of First Order
definitions.definition: A linear differential equation of first order is of the form dy/dx + Py = Q where P and Q are functions of x only.
A general solution of a differential equation is a solution that contains arbitrary constants.
A particular solution of a differential equation is a solution that satisfies the given initial condition.
The auxiliary equation is obtained by substituting y = e^(mx) in the given differential equation.
The complementary function is the solution of the auxiliary equation.
The particular integral is the particular solution of the differential equation.
In this method, the particular integral is assumed to be a polynomial or a product of a polynomial and e^(ax) or a product of e^(ax) and a polynomial.
In this method, the particular integral is assumed to be a linear combination of the solution of the homogeneous equation.
A differential equation is said to be separable if it can be written in the form M(x,y)dx + N(x,y)dy=0 where M(x,y) and N(x,y) are functions of x and y only.
A differential equation is said to be homogeneous if it can be written in the form f(x,y)/y=g(x,y).
Bernoulli's equation is a non-linear differential equation of the form dy/dx+P(x)y=Q(x)y^n.
A slope field is a graphical representation of the solution of a differential equation.
